agents.name: Planner
agents.lastMessage: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific int

report.hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212), while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will disrupt off-target SSTR3/5 electrostatic interactions without compromising SSTR2 binding.

agents.name: Critic
agents.lastMessage: The current design strategy is effective at achieving strong SSTR2 binding but i

report.hypothesis: The current design strategy is effective at achieving strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient at ensuring selectivity. Future iterations must prioritize disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while maintaining the pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing design efforts here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore core.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the overall peptide fold and pharmacophore integrity.
